_**By David Shuster**_
As I write this column, I am boiling over with anger and contempt: not merely at Donald Trump, but at the terrifying spectacle of all the people in our nation who are still willing to tolerate him. Trump has gone beyond erratic demagogue. He has now degenerated into a buffoon king with the temperament of a toddler and the moral compass of a blood thirsty psychopath.
On this day, Donald Trump stooped to a new low so appalling that even his most ardent White House enablers and Congressional sycophants are squirming.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5e4a33d5-4ad1-4004-8068-d4a70a5688a7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Trump labeled Six democratic members of Congress (two democratic Senators and four democratic house members) "traitors" and demanded they be "ARRESTED AND PUT ON TRIAL" -- and then Trump declared their actions "punishable by DEATH."
Let that sink in: the President of the United States, the man sworn to uphold ad protect the Constitution, is publicly associating with calls for political murder -- not figurative murder, but the very real incitement of execution.
This is not a mere policy squabble. The six lawmakers -- veterans and national-security professionals -- released a solemn video message to members of the U.S. military: "You can refuse illegal orders ... You must refuse illegal orders."
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/6d0053b6-d824-43b4-a82e-daad36312b46?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The lawmakers were not fomenting violence; they were reminding Americans in uniform of their highest duty: fidelity to the Constitution.
What does Trump do? He melts down on Truth Social, shrieking that their statement is "seditious behavior at the highest level" and that "an example MUST BE SET." Then Trump escalate by sharing posts calling for the lawmakers to be punished by hanging.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/b7e5c081-5b54-4374-af8e-3ab4676fb35c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Since the beginning of this great American experiment and continuing through this day, countless cynics have described our Democracy as a pathetic belief in the collective wisdom of individual ignorance. By that measure, Trump's rise and power are a grim joke. But the most recent Trump declarations are not humor. They are a threat. They are not just the rantings of a madman. They are the manifesto of a man who seeks to be an authoritarian strongman.
It is not enough for us to merely deplore and condemn this behavior. We must act. The House of Representatives -- if it has any spine -- must immediately begin impeachment proceedings. The Senate -- to salvage whatever remains of its institutional credibility -- must convict and expel this malignant farce from the Presidency.
* * *
Blue Amp Media is a reader-supported publication--please consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.G38t5-E4EsEhVpftTHsqdE4kCKDTUM8dxu1UeMHFDGA?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=179511405)
* * *
Why? Because we are now witnessing the collapse of the most basic restraint the Framers built into our system: the idea that even the highest officer in the land, the President, must respect due process, uphold the law, and refrain from lethal vendettas. The founders gave us impeachment precisely for this kind of moral and constitutional crisis.
I get that some MAGA lawmakers are already excusing Trump's incitement and are lying about the democrats' video.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2cfcd83c-f7cf-4402-bf5a-0529c37fc06e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
House Speaker Mike Johnson said it was "dangerous for leading members of Congress to tell troops to disobey orders." Johnson knows that's not what the democrats said. The democrats told the troops to disobey UNLAWFUL orders."
Countless conservative legal analysts agree with the democrats somber message. Ed Whelan, a senior fellow at the Ethics and Public Policy Center wrote, "disobeying an unlawful order is required." Fox News legal analyst Andrew McCarthy wrote that even if an order is lawful and disobeyed it's not sedition. He wrote, "Disobeying a lawful order is insubordination, not insurrection or sedition. Disobeying an unlawful order is required."
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/6f4d7f73-6cc1-4e37-8e7d-3cc032288c1d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d43c299e-c132-4880-8050-e5973f9f9dbc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
There are some democrats who will oppose impeachment for pure political reasons. If Trump sticks around, the democrats are headed towards landslide victories in the midterm elections. But that calculation is not worth the price of allowing a President so contemptuous of democratic norms and so lavish in his threats to remain in power. No legislative victory, no policy focus can compensate for a President who openly fantasizes about executing his political adversaries.
Let's be clear. This is not politics-as-usual. This is not a mere Trump rhetorical slip or unfortunate hyperbole. This is a watershed moment. If we accept this without consequence, we all but guarantee that the next escalation -- or the one after that -- will be worse.
Donald Trump's Presidency has been a disgrace from the start. Every word, every deed, every breath he has taken has been in service of his self-interest, his ego, and his vendettas.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e9df63d3-6432-4a52-8e2f-28f3c54b4a3f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
But we now have a moment so clear, so morally and legally wrong, that to fail to act would be a betrayal to everything America and our constitution stand for.
So, let Congress do its job. Let the House draw up the articles. Let the Senate vote to convict. Remove this would-be executioner from office -- **today** -- before Trump normalizes and further encourages political murder.
History will judge us. Our democracy demands an act of bravery. We can not wait any longer.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/6f4d7f73-6cc1-4e37-8e7d-3cc032288c1d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---

name: James Talarico
subject: Gas money
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Talarico for Texas
body:
#
Peter --
We’re barnstorming Texas — meeting people where they are to get out our message and talk about how to take back our state for the people.
We’ve just finished our 20th event in just 2 months, and the response has been overwhelming. We’re showing up in places where people haven’t seen a statewide Democratic candidate in years.
Folks say they’ve never seen anything like this. Nearly a year from Election Day, we’re packing rooms in cities and rural towns alike. And at nearly every one of our events, more people show up than RSVP — something virtually unheard of on political campaigns.
[](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-launch.bF5RK3&refcode=em251123-1-ak&t=1&akid=253%2E1347274%2EPd6Ods)
Even when we prepare for a larger turnout, our overflow has had overflow. It’s incredible.
But doing all these events is no small feat. I’ve put tens of thousands of miles on my pickup truck driving across the state since launching this campaign.
[](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-launch.bF5RK3&refcode=em251123-1-ak&t=2&akid=253%2E1347274%2EPd6Ods)
[**Today, I am asking for your help to stay on the road. Can you make a contribution to help us put gas in the tank? Anything you can give will help us keep showing up everywhere.**](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-launch.bF5RK3&refcode=em251123-1-ak&t=3&akid=253%2E1347274%2EPd6Ods)
[DONATE](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-launch.bF5RK3&refcode=em251123-1-ak&t=4&akid=253%2E1347274%2EPd6Ods)
James

Paid for by Talarico for Texas
Talarico for Texas
PO Box 14508
Austin, TX 78761
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you need to update your contact information, [**you can submit this form.**](https://act.jamestalarico.com/signup/update-info/?t=5&akid=253%2E1347274%2EPd6Ods) Looking for Team Talarico merch? [**Check out our online store!**](https://store.jamestalarico.com?utm_source=em_footer&utm_campaign=em_footer) This is a grassroots movement to take back Texas for working people. [**Please consider making a contribution to support our fight today.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jt-em-launch?refcode=em_footer&t=6&refcode2=253_1347274_Pd6Ods&akid=253%2E1347274%2EPd6Ods)
James is grateful to have you in our movement. We try to send only the most important updates and opportunities to participate via email, but if you want to, you can [unsubscribe here](https://act.jamestalarico.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=7&akid=253%2E1347274%2EPd6Ods).

name: Tina Kotek
subject: I’m fighting with everything I’ve got
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Tina Kotek
body:
When Trump plays despicable games to hurt us, I fight back. |
#
---
Is there anything I can say to you, Peter, to convince you to chip in $5 today? I’m fighting with everything I’ve got to protect our state from President Trump. When he played despicable games with people’s ability to put food on their tables, I made sure Oregonians wouldn’t go hungry. And I’m working tirelessly to push back against Trump’s overreach, **but the truth is I need resources to keep up this difficult fight.** [_**Will you make a donation now? I could really use your support during these tough times.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119840799/584400488/-780220320?refcode=AU_TK_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251123_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZmU0MTdlMS04OWM4LWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UoSkeFYSAft-ZhAAWDi_CnKhALTMsV4NONkIdbuYPbU=) Thank you. clean_body: When Trump plays despicable games to hurt us, I fight back. | Is there anything I can say to you, Peter, to convince you to chip in $5 today? I’m fighting with everything I’ve got to protect our state from President Trump. When he played despicable games with people’s ability to put food on their tables, I made sure Oregonians wouldn’t go hungry. And I’m working tirelessly to push back against Trump’s overreach, but the truth is I need resources to keep up this difficult fight. = Thank you. Tina | = Tina for Oregon PO Box 42307 Portland, OR 97242 | mailto:info@tinafororegon.com | Paid for by Friends of Tina Kotek

[I’d like to tell you a quick story.](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=295835943f2248bbaf8ab7acd20b57fe&_e=Rt1LMVMJF16CGpbyftF8JjfnLRNxC3C2KWQ-65dz6Vngccfo2bGv-UyqRSwahc-Z4xwm3x0m35qEo8pEinWdtbtXKkASP6LSzFwdXd4CU0L4FSoCqbmTIuCz-WwLe6A1s_7ZVrk9bDblnaCEkwLFqQjnbgr4o7Nq662sCQKFBvBzS-Gg-v_7xEZFXcUavadfZlQ_Yq8M1KJgodZKKn2sO5X2pH_tEZxg8belknnVuO87Oaiyo0E7V5paiBpPuiMT1pJ4YE7tEWgfAp5PSmItZVCbmI6cCObD-MOr0PwfY6tEDFn7GU2OeR7t6-nkZiMF0JZrS6gHElxspiOyclfqdWmBUrmQlY_SItkIuFcS8e6j03kG-ng521UuwWIYqwept8KlC7x7CqGtsyyplxVk6RllSlZFYNrt8axo1K5ZW_0ZAyFV9q7ps14P0PSnUIe9JSRBjMAswvBsncl_sypycW9-D-L8TiJBYsYJWdQrYTsVxvaQAeHTXggGDahPuzNfiQEnF7ZbALJndgr3QHP1f2L8cuZsb9tz2EW8dzwEyAsnVvQybCuoMiK28tJwLZMjtBTcRKfw-tgi4KeS3ZXKmMcByclXQmPdivnXEhGpwh-6jowWVbjnnpWnCl3DGche9Eq7fl8cA_xxoWF0R5JwQz5pbroXiQvT3MoLorYqe3OpCE3QmMf08Z9_t3_WU3Tp39mElxxI9y6bM7IaGowg0pLu2NjGNQ2sGcpQvxtt9O-R9S_aXVuDl13MWwFglJwRKQiMRQDNfwwyuX6cj1E2MJWWPtWlmYQoIBUnr4eoRjMrpYC-MVMHBq4KyCgOdzYg8cY2Vj9k4ekRyQeKrS4IKurLNgJJ6JxXLA0ji1FbtmhpOb_q9o-NRYhI-xT5iFf3Xpd4D9-lRMLSIX8ElhxT2e0uGLbnPMwedNMaXuxzcpA%3D)
I grew up right here in Ohio. My parents worked hard to give me a chance at the American Dream, and I know what sacrifice looks like. Every opportunity I've had was built on the back of that sacrifice.[](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=295835943f2248bbaf8ab7acd20b57fe&_e=Rt1LMVMJF16CGpbyftF8JjfnLRNxC3C2KWQ-65dz6Vngccfo2bGv-UyqRSwahc-Z4xwm3x0m35qEo8pEinWdtbtXKkASP6LSzFwdXd4CU0L4FSoCqbmTIuCz-WwLe6A1s_7ZVrk9bDblnaCEkwLFqQjnbgr4o7Nq662sCQKFBvBzS-Gg-v_7xEZFXcUavadfZlQ_Yq8M1KJgodZKKn2sO5X2pH_tEZxg8belknnVuO87Oaiyo0E7V5paiBpPuiMT1pJ4YE7tEWgfAp5PSmItZVCbmI6cCObD-MOr0PwfY6tEDFn7GU2OeR7t6-nkZiMF0JZrS6gHElxspiOyclfqdWmBUrmQlY_SItkIuFcS8e7t0lm8a48LRgBOUqdJ4TsCcvEuW7Lu7M8KZBGKxnVYx3-vkiosGCbDCaROKa5kkpr2OqRv-PYCH-3CuePHE3M19HeoOG6LN1mWzQo1GtUp8ps9hH7KBiIIDNcm0gKRje1EVwLfhAAMkUpmmhKdxICM29DyVl_7yyrHAwCoa9-GLtxPWdw1vWngO0K73Tvl_GCcNkcr-yc2nn5oe_Lue1hYaEXzJX-kFrJUV8BSio9Vu5Ozxu9YRatHMvPaxFcZSY1pAQrGW3LtRf_YxtEz7buYsuj_v9OBfWq8RLQ7XUUs5GMfIFsBVxYe45wfXMLfXaw-Pnqp_Hcmm4cgftzkSyucnpREE5E7Kspz8kfLtt9q1KoX9Y38g4Z6mhoL5a4Kac1cYYNZZ3hOU6bnb1QFwIBwC10YsHMldOmu6Jfzt39Mfby_Io5XPAsC1zh8m9BZ3gLept90gx19MD27zYLyJ-uEUHhUOagZvRAAdGE4ZDYi3qKkogkkH-pqr6SFq9QHv0J6Vl7yHkNu-m6E9dFNCs2RFQfVhSFZnlvhhyDksz1XuCOIHAVCsgstfzx0yA08sNs%3D)That's why I never forget what it means when someone like you chips in a few dollars. It isn't "just money." **
**It 's trust. It's belief. It's a vote of confidence in the movement we're building in Ohio and beyond. **
**So whether you 've already donated or you're considering it today, let me say this clearly: **
**[THANK YOU, Fellow American!](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=295835943f2248bbaf8ab7acd20b57fe&_e=Rt1LMVMJF16CGpbyftF8JjfnLRNxC3C2KWQ-65dz6Vngccfo2bGv-UyqRSwahc-Z4xwm3x0m35qEo8pEinWdtbtXKkASP6LSzFwdXd4CU0L4FSoCqbmTIuCz-WwLe6A1s_7ZVrk9bDblnaCEkwLFqQjnbgr4o7Nq662sCQKFBvBzS-Gg-v_7xEZFXcUavadfZlQ_Yq8M1KJgodZKKn2sO5X2pH_tEZxg8belknnVuO87Oaiyo0E7V5paiBpPuiMT1pJ4YE7tEWgfAp5PSmItZVCbmI6cCObD-MOr0PwfY6tEDFn7GU2OeR7t6-nkZiMF0JZrS6gHElxspiOyclfqdWmBUrmQlY_SItkIuFcS8e7cQqCHG5qHad9A-lg1cNRnGPBP8fpCvwkzrKi9IgUciHLmm4E5B5nPLbppbTY3x57d7xo3sqBqwacPraSBvnmLAmZ56m5_lkyUKe1GAwgPjgjhqRGeQ4c2JuLAOzjDJfcYOIpMQXEfePBuUbfV70HQO511OBEP7I48kxiDLqnpWOsQs7ckoT21K-PB2EJYkMPEOUvk0Vl8NjSnkpi387ecpvSeT0clfuozQB44j3Zi9trUpbHgisOFS4Rx61vWPou2HTAmBf6oNj2Pgt47jdd_6zzORRTXhz3kzDngz--UX9bvfCT3isvk4dv1NNdWAuC-aeeyDRlRIHcOeQo3G5tjKNi65WkwY48ADxYXkr2bpWeJ_SR6jYiYAhpu9X5jSoFG4VIuWn3CTBHJ_u0JxMinvoLs3TRGpHjDMR3k2gpOedAFKPIQ7se7H_WTG3To6oTv_Yf5bwsUjORl4saeNsfVfXfXhdFF6iqYbz0pnrdsPNPFrIEo2IM4qzgkFHXpGw_r_rA5CXrG3s2TYPuDT-sl8GhlVG-5LHp_s5FmlloWT7gTpA9K0NawN0-7f7tUkjM%3D)**
**We 're in an extremely tough fight. And this isn't just any race - it's shaping up to be one of the most important in the country.**
**I threw my hat in the ring now because we have a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to save our country. I mean that. We were[ ONE ELECTION AWAY](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=295835943f2248bbaf8ab7acd20b57fe&_e=Rt1LMVMJF16CGpbyftF8JjfnLRNxC3C2KWQ-65dz6Vngccfo2bGv-UyqRSwahc-Z4xwm3x0m35qEo8pEinWdtbtXKkASP6LSzFwdXd4CU0L4FSoCqbmTIuCz-WwLe6A1s_7ZVrk9bDblnaCEkwLFqQjnbgr4o7Nq662sCQKFBvBzS-Gg-v_7xEZFXcUavadfZlQ_Yq8M1KJgodZKKn2sO5X2pH_tEZxg8belknnVuO87Oaiyo0E7V5paiBpPuiMT1pJ4YE7tEWgfAp5PSmItZVCbmI6cCObD-MOr0PwfY6tEDFn7GU2OeR7t6-nkZiMF0JZrS6gHElxspiOyclfqdWmBUrmQlY_SItkIuFcS8e5X6K9hjH9cAFHmU3lAv7uYrm9CkxDRME1Rf2FCSajPUhm0bX-d3FIu_kvycfCvHXx_ritRPbbaTv0YOkGHvV9IEv2ScBydXwmfP8VzFcVLKWTa9BbFRnYLdQoV4VHxR8NC7B8qImUymtYa_-MlWP51_OUAopAMEG70xHf79luM2mnNVfqKXUOcjmuWryelTi_wscz-exNC6emnnd6FfnZHpa42u6jtgQ3908CM1NQQeykEK97ibHfiVlOWOferLzIUyelFGSgKIgEpJ3_cNrOJOmC3MDqIxJxlyuy0ZmdkekBx6eDxsHTmL1jW3JlEeC93hG2pZ9uoHfPUf9MSOZ1PoPkO_r1LVt-d-eNTAkzG1r8XqhbmNfREBfsvD-ncxqsGc6Gg4qEpvGeXZp1cXicKiI-Y-0m04LQ3RGmkHtWbvWypziN-qBcgJe4lLd8HjOJUaPEAoH7iK55nlO-1vZyzW2-aIp7bVBrtJK7EwwNF0KDFD3_EQbvhmd_YMzL4wRHfGHB45aAQ0GBsndPumT2uW0xkietqI1xcbIxFLduXlGVLB7msNPPgLLmFJjlUfWU%3D) from a country you and I wouldn't recognize anymore.**
**President Trump saved us - with the help of folks like you - from that dire outcome. With that said, he needs loyal partners at the state level to help him succeed. **
**That 's why this race means so much. **
**So if you 've given before (even just once, even just a buck) know this: you've already made a difference, and I will never take your sacrifice for granted. **
**And if you haven 't given yet - [and you’re able to spare even $1 today](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=295835943f2248bbaf8ab7acd20b57fe&_e=Rt1LMVMJF16CGpbyftF8JjfnLRNxC3C2KWQ-65dz6Vngccfo2bGv-UyqRSwahc-Z4xwm3x0m35qEo8pEinWdtbtXKkASP6LSzFwdXd4CU0L4FSoCqbmTIuCz-WwLe6A1s_7ZVrk9bDblnaCEkwLFqQjnbgr4o7Nq662sCQKFBvBzS-Gg-v_7xEZFXcUavadfZlQ_Yq8M1KJgodZKKn2sO5X2pH_tEZxg8belknnVuO87Oaiyo0E7V5paiBpPuiMT1pJ4YE7tEWgfAp5PSmItZVCbmI6cCObD-MOr0PwfY6tEDFn7GU2OeR7t6-nkZiMF0JZrS6gHElxspiOyclfqdWmBUrmQlY_SItkIuFcS8e5FfMa6lgvA_hyLkEGvhlCD4u9Um6h8qTTo7QS_yvdFTd64PnbfyW_Do7Xl0iE9zX5tRNeBqXNn1iDX7piTZnqcYq8d_pbUkJC2UPv5v3INRLqeKnYAmYufLx0UJjKfMGXv_WO4RtxKRjrOKgJgQxMoU55vGgfT94rpkdIzoDLuqa7qcZyYHQomk503EtMedEXM6isXlwRAn1vy9lo4OXUyV4mZJYD4suRoVBH4Iy_qoop6PCzvNUVOiMmB6Z_JF5uhQ4dLkDjDm8zIHNIP4MsUK8unQo-vmHrbl0OnpCnIqi1OGHOESERvJAsjN-ZgXX05Nf9lPxLCjs6m0EgDgkXImWJe1Abgi49hKud4e0RMQFQKuahzEjpjlYQZEs8_XZFlIT06U6nkKJNj7fQ7lqtD_iVve8ZnWqlrvlo_09SgS3zhKdLbPJiu0CKaHukAY42G5Q-mn_PtHcmZM26rt8Ol8bSI4YhdTNql7mA4R0ypcEgB-bgFuEbiHgTD6gmSTeJP_eYg7RwWcR_65g386YvFp9lp-AeiZABRLxmxc-VFDMgTIFUDvtVi-xU84hGGKr0%3D) - I'd be honored to have you on my team.

clean_body: I will never take your sacrifice for granted. today is full of noise - phony deadlines, scare tactics, and cheap gimmicks. But I want to cut through the noise for a moment. I grew up right here in Ohio. My parents worked hard to give me a chance at the American Dream, and I know what sacrifice looks like. Every opportunity I've had was built on the back of that sacrifice. why I never forget what it means when someone like you chips in a few dollars. It isn't "just money." It 's trust. It's belief. It's a vote of confidence in the movement we're building in Ohio and beyond. So whether you 've already donated or you're considering it today, let me say this clearly: We 're in an extremely tough fight. And this isn't just any race - it's shaping up to be one of the most important in the country. I threw my hat in the ring now because we have a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to save our country. I mean that. We were from a country you and I wouldn't recognize anymore. President Trump saved us - with the help of folks like you - from that dire outcome. With that said, he needs loyal partners at the state level to help him succeed. That 's why this race means so much. So if you 've given before (even just once, even just a buck) know this: you've already made a difference, and I will never take your sacrifice for granted. And if you haven 't given yet - - I'd be honored to have you on my team. Friend, **This week I introduced a new resolution in Congress to formally recognize the genocide of Palestinians in Gaza and end our country’s complicity in it.** [**Can you sign our petition today to support this resolution and demand Congress stop supporting genocide?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm3-inXg7XQKmpx2SINKyl6WQz_vkJ3ZJKcUzkkSwqwfKGU-DTKGBSUC0Zo0ncv5Ir7D2OqlrrUB3J2azuZObaLZLGo_MdnRwBJZSgn3qr1AYRv-1fvUyzGDpMQ32ycK2FVWeozd8TDHg8oUcvdYwtpDXLOhVxzpCDCY05egVoTt5BN9jHKCefLMzfAC08BTxBPYQQG9rZXJS9hBD5YRRv-TNkPBACJLsIvFOLBc_M4DyPOYoefpYpt_WmpZd0UDT6qqW3eqG8PkHSfn7C6wiqsLbHefORXHakFdZxld59pnnPytBBCyVJT_xqlozF85cUqi9P9v9tyPmOorVD8GpGvryxN0bsE4x_oCX69uLCxqTlRrbcs_02gkjdrTtIVl4IQTx-pyx08Bo9wOYrwlekGmn/4lu/0LUGL3xyTLmskhwA7Q3Lsw/h1/h001.lyAf3Xrzumn0HbSaFb2rsHvJ6zGW6K5qi-HOZ_LHec4) Sponsored by 20 of my colleagues in the House of Representatives, **this resolution lays out evidence showing that Israel’s actions meet the legal definition of genocide under the Genocide Convention** —including the Israeli military’s own data, which says that 83% of Palestinians killed in the past two years have been civilians. **The resolution demands the U.S. follow its obligations to prevent and punish the crime of genocide** —including by ending weapons transfers, stopping military aid, sanctioning Israeli officials, and holding other perpetrators of this genocide to account. House Resolution 876 also notes that our government has sent $52 billion of our tax dollars to Israel’s genocidal military over the past two years, through military aid and weapons transfers. And **now, the Trump administration is negotiating with Israel’s government to send an additional $76 billion worth of weapons** , paid for with our tax dollars. I’m standing with the majority of Americans in demanding our government stop funding and arming the Israeli government’s genocide of Palestinians. Will you stand with us today? [**Add your name to demand Congress recognize the genocide in Gaza and end U.S. complicity in mass atrocities against the Palestinian people.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm3-inXg7XQKmpx2SINKyl6WQz_vkJ3ZJKcUzkkSwqwfKGU-DTKGBSUC0Zo0ncv5Ir7D2OqlrrUB3J2azuZObaLZLGo_MdnRwBJZSgn3qr1AYRv-1fvUyzGDpMQ32ycK2FVWeozd8TDHg8oUcvdYwtpDXLOhVxzpCDCY05egVoTt5BGi6ofHbWXLSUAj--ENfbWyFalLWlkTPp6n-Vix0g-SZX4OvQVrRWbs-bivB33MblIE4cJeYBuee93Uq20bHq0_3s8qCl0WL76iupYtCnOPbJpjgBxwPcVlBPCNjrZ0qFjZLi24InZXVUI_eG3tL4dQ6KPplmSE6gdBreL7yjRIbW49z6QK-6yk_qPzkZvA7faKunMvZAsS7G_YXbWHMdQpH-9vAWbGwBOjyh3sTuOJ9/4lu/0LUGL3xyTLmskhwA7Q3Lsw/h2/h001.wzef79larOIedrtCrwqrunkY8D-m0kQIu5a0AfG187Q) | [ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm3-inXg7XQKmpx2SINKyl6WQz_vkJ3ZJKcUzkkSwqwfKGU-DTKGBSUC0Zo0ncv5Ir7D2OqlrrUB3J2azuZObaLZLGo_MdnRwBJZSgn3qr1AYRv-1fvUyzGDpMQ32ycK2FVWeozd8TDHg8oUcvdYwtpDXLOhVxzpCDCY05egVoTt5BKiWacXOqZSzNdBSUgzBhg8sBZ41zdzqv63mW0UjSkFA0PVcGEoXQnq9neYr6n0Xav24Giza5mtIxOcpCMlpWoQnHLAiMMU40TVKbFkbXZ5J9n747z4IvG4mrhRKBNgXAPv7WhDjF08xE71YtodVWqc3iq-cmcDdMIIKqRJ-bdI06E9fD7zdIix9lWHh15aMVZVMPUPe7zqaFqGUgXatv_o2TLGLCOZQuklIQgVniVK8/4lu/0LUGL3xyTLmskhwA7Q3Lsw/h3/h001.8Itg5UVWVJgNwO8kgKuy44v8r2hBeJGHu4HIrt02PI4)

Israel’s government has continually violated its so-called “ceasefire” in Gaza. And this week, the UN Security Council outrageously voted to increase Trump’s control over the U.S.-Israeli occupation of Gaza—further undermining Palestinians’ legal right to self-determination.
Already, Israel’s government has illegal control over Gaza: As intense flooding destroys the tents of hundreds of thousands of forcibly displaced Palestinians with nowhere to go, Israel’s government is blocking emergency shelter as well as food from entering Gaza. This week’s UN Security Council vote further entrenches this occupation.
Our government must urgently take action to reverse course. As the Political Director of Jewish Voice for Peace Action affirmed when endorsing my new resolution:
> _“For over two years, the U.S. has been a full partner in the Israeli government’s genocide against Palestinians. Presidents and members of Congress have denied and erased Israel’s ongoing atrocities in Gaza, shielded Israel from accountability in the international arena, and attempted to dehumanize Palestinians.”_
She added:_“Congresswoman Tlaib and the original co-sponsors joining her on this historic resolution are making clear that this complicity must come to an end.”_
[**Please join us and the majority of Americans demanding an end to this genocide: Add your name to support my resolution today.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm3-inXg7XQKmpx2SINKyl6WQz_vkJ3ZJKcUzkkSwqwfKGU-DTKGBSUC0Zo0ncv5Ir7D2OqlrrUB3J2azuZObaLZLGo_MdnRwBJZSgn3qr1AYRv-1fvUyzGDpMQ32ycK2FVWeozd8TDHg8oUcvdYwtpDXLOhVxzpCDCY05egVoTt5BGHOrW6uQ0ZogWdasIh_3mdGYryMrzM5JugrtG3POXKc-N9M026Pg3hO5-W_h1YkK0W7TbcVF83cwZLQQZH28WQ5PcD_l6eP0dp56NZOOoC2A-z01DNh0znh3duF42F4Dzw8JLTjtYjyJqACzBWZoxntE7FiEeZN3U3a-ep_jKRFMuqnSDfQvY1Jk-ZIm-wYgN3XTQ0MUeL94MF1is23aKKWSHeItFN4tYuWGTVA2-zk/4lu/0LUGL3xyTLmskhwA7Q3Lsw/h4/h001.Yg7hhxKLBiTj2731kD4Aolvxl8fizvRnN2mJinUs0yo)
Thank you. Together, we’ll keep fighting state violence and demanding human rights be respected—at home and abroad.
With you in the fight for life, peace, and justice,
Rashida

Friend,
This week, the United Nations Security Council voted to give the U.S. and Israel more control to illegally occupy Gaza, further stripping away Palestinians’ right to self-determination.
This plan—which former senior UN human rights official Craig Mokhiber called a “colonial outrage”—grants Israel even more impunity to continue mass atrocities. Meanwhile, Israel’s government continues violently violating its “ceasefire” in Gaza and surrounding countries.
**We can’t let this defiance of international law continue.** We have a moral and legal responsibility to reject genocide denial, call this what it is, and end our country’s complicity.
So **I introduced a new resolution in Congress to officially recognize the genocide of Palestinians in Gaza** and demand the U.S. follow its binding obligations to do all it can to prevent genocide.

Joining over 100 organizations endorsing this resolution, the human rights group Amnesty International USA wrote:
> **_“Amnesty International calls on all members of Congress to urgently support this resolution and ensure the U.S. begins taking the actions necessary to prevent and punish Israel’s genocide in Gaza.”_**
Twenty of my colleagues in Congress have co-sponsored my resolution, House Resolution 876, which:
* **Documents overwhelming evidence establishing that the Israeli government has committed genocide** and lays out how its military and government have also demonstrated a clear pattern of genocidal intent at the highest levels
* **Demands the U.S. follow international law and its obligations (as a party to the Genocide Convention) to prevent and punish the crime of genocide** —including by ending weapons transfers, stopping military aid, and sanctioning Israeli officials
* **Calls for investigating and prosecuting** U.S. individuals and corporations that have participated in this genocide
* **Calls on the U.S. to cooperate with the International Criminal Court and the United Nations** to hold Israeli perpetrators accountable
* **Pushes to restore UNRWA funding** to support desperately-needed humanitarian aid for Palestinians
* Notes that in the past two years, the U.S. has sent nearly $22 billion in military aid to Israel and authorized over $30 billion in weapons deals

That’s **$52 billion American taxpayer dollars funding the slaughter of Palestinians** , while most Americans live paycheck to paycheck and struggle to pay for basic needs like housing, food, and healthcare.
After announcing that he’s forcing the 42 million Americans on SNAP to reapply for food stamps before they can get food assistance, Trump is now negotiating with Israel’s war criminal Prime Minister—who’s demanding a new weapons deal that would cost U.S. taxpayers over $76 billion.
Congress must act to stop these illegal weapons giveaways immediately.
[**Please add your name to demand Congress fulfill our country’s legal obligations to bring this genocide to an end, and hold the perpetrators accountable for their atrocities against Palestinians.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm3-inXg7XQKmpx2SINKyl6WQz_vkJ3ZJKcUzkkSwqwfKGU-DTKGBSUC0Zo0ncv5Ir7D2OqlrrUB3J2azuZObaLZLGo_MdnRwBJZSgn3qr1AYRv-1fvUyzGDpMQ32ycK2FVWeozd8TDHg8oUcvdYwtpDXLOhVxzpCDCY05egVoTt5BGEC42u3tgjrv8vAP3dc5yqXTlOatj5Zf6IM2aqSVdfGcMfkjfENoJoOOBsNfUJdqlkSYt0w6FxOEtIJXZELq2djNkuwhwKvd02xwCPZf_YHPFWb93JpjnKroSvRKnwayYJPKX99YkrEDhblx_XTaYJ62tSAb-qK8UTCPEbMWYKrZArUa2k27lQzztp39AkJzdNJRu0Cduch3_tDfoEVfZ2lx50a9dD4TQ1CxuAWQOXD/4lu/0LUGL3xyTLmskhwA7Q3Lsw/h7/h001.4u7ZA_s6NKhdqhIrOEQ7kkhJpZy4z_V_I3yzZ5yNwFU)

Thank you for fighting for the rights and human dignity of Palestinians and all people.
We must keep pushing to end the violent occupation of Palestine, including the West Bank and East Jerusalem.
Last year, **the International Court of Justice ruled that Israel must end its unlawful occupation** , including illegal settlements, and withdraw from Gaza and the rest of Palestine “as rapidly as possible.”
The world’s top court added that Israel is violating the international prohibition against apartheid—and also mandated Israel to abide by international law and ensure Palestinians’ rights to reparations and return.
These rulings were unfortunately non-binding, so the United States and other governments must step in to enforce them. This is part of governments’ duty to uphold international law.
We will keep building the pressure and the power needed to change our country. I’m honored to fight alongside you for equity and liberation for all.
With you in the fight,
Rashida

clean_body: I introduced a new resolution in Congress to officially recognize the genocide of Palestinians in Gaza and demand the U.S. follow its binding obligations to do all it can to prevent genocide. | | | Friend, This week I introduced a new resolution in Congress to formally recognize the genocide of Palestinians in Gaza and end our country’s complicity in it. Sponsored by 20 of my colleagues in the House of Representatives, this resolution lays out evidence showing that Israel’s actions meet the legal definition of genocide under the Genocide Convention —including the Israeli military’s own data, which says that 83% of Palestinians killed in the past two years have been civilians. The resolution demands the U.S. follow its obligations to prevent and punish the crime of genocide —including by ending weapons transfers, stopping military aid, sanctioning Israeli officials, and holding other perpetrators of this genocide to account. House Resolution 876 also notes that our government has sent $52 billion of our tax dollars to Israel’s genocidal military over the past two years, through military aid and weapons transfers. And now, the Trump administration is negotiating with Israel’s government to send an additional $76 billion worth of weapons , paid for with our tax dollars. I’m standing with the majority of Americans in demanding our government stop funding and arming the Israeli government’s genocide of Palestinians. Will you stand with us today? | Israel’s government has continually violated its so-called “ceasefire” in Gaza. And this week, the UN Security Council outrageously voted to increase Trump’s control over the U.S.-Israeli occupation of Gaza—further undermining Palestinians’ legal right to self-determination. Already, Israel’s government has illegal control over Gaza: As intense flooding destroys the tents of hundreds of thousands of forcibly displaced Palestinians with nowhere to go, Israel’s government is blocking emergency shelter as well as food from entering Gaza. This week’s UN Security Council vote further entrenches this occupation. Our government must urgently take action to reverse course. As the Political Director of Jewish Voice for Peace Action affirmed when endorsing my new resolution: “For over two years, the U.S. has been a full partner in the Israeli government’s genocide against Palestinians. Presidents and members of Congress have denied and erased Israel’s ongoing atrocities in Gaza, shielded Israel from accountability in the international arena, and attempted to dehumanize Palestinians.” She added:“Congresswoman Tlaib and the original co-sponsors joining her on this historic resolution are making clear that this complicity must come to an end.” Thank you. Together, we’ll keep fighting state violence and demanding human rights be respected—at home and abroad. With you in the fight for life, peace, and justice, Rashida \---------- Forwarded message --------- From: Rashida Tlaib** Date: Sat, Nov 22, 2025 Subject: Pressure Congress to stop supporting genocide and abide by international law To: dpwillis67@gmail.com Friend, This week, the United Nations Security Council voted to give the U.S. and Israel more control to illegally occupy Gaza, further stripping away Palestinians’ right to self-determination. This plan—which former senior UN human rights official Craig Mokhiber called a “colonial outrage”—grants Israel even more impunity to continue mass atrocities. Meanwhile, Israel’s government continues violently violating its “ceasefire” in Gaza and surrounding countries. We can’t let this defiance of international law continue. We have a moral and legal responsibility to reject genocide denial, call this what it is, and end our country’s complicity. So I introduced a new resolution in Congress to officially recognize the genocide of Palestinians in Gaza and demand the U.S. follow its binding obligations to do all it can to prevent genocide. Joining over 100 organizations endorsing this resolution, the human rights group Amnesty International USA wrote: “Amnesty International calls on all members of Congress to urgently support this resolution and ensure the U.S. begins taking the actions necessary to prevent and punish Israel’s genocide in Gaza.” Twenty of my colleagues in Congress have co-sponsored my resolution, House Resolution 876, which: Documents overwhelming evidence establishing that the Israeli government has committed genocide and lays out how its military and government have also demonstrated a clear pattern of genocidal intent at the highest levels Demands the U.S. follow international law and its obligations (as a party to the Genocide Convention) to prevent and punish the crime of genocide —including by ending weapons transfers, stopping military aid, and sanctioning Israeli officials Calls for investigating and prosecuting U.S. individuals and corporations that have participated in this genocide Calls on the U.S. to cooperate with the International Criminal Court and the United Nations to hold Israeli perpetrators accountable Pushes to restore UNRWA funding to support desperately-needed humanitarian aid for Palestinians Notes that in the past two years, the U.S. has sent nearly $22 billion in military aid to Israel and authorized over $30 billion in weapons deals That’s $52 billion American taxpayer dollars funding the slaughter of Palestinians , while most Americans live paycheck to paycheck and struggle to pay for basic needs like housing, food, and healthcare. After announcing that he’s forcing the 42 million Americans on SNAP to reapply for food stamps before they can get food assistance, Trump is now negotiating with Israel’s war criminal Prime Minister—who’s demanding a new weapons deal that would cost U.S. taxpayers over $76 billion. Congress must act to stop these illegal weapons giveaways immediately. Thank you for fighting for the rights and human dignity of Palestinians and all people. We must keep pushing to end the violent occupation of Palestine, including the West Bank and East Jerusalem. Last year, the International Court of Justice ruled that Israel must end its unlawful occupation , including illegal settlements, and withdraw from Gaza and the rest of Palestine “as rapidly as possible.” The world’s top court added that Israel is violating the international prohibition against apartheid—and also mandated Israel to abide by international law and ensure Palestinians’ rights to reparations and return. These rulings were unfortunately non-binding, so the United States and other governments must step in to enforce them. This is part of governments’ duty to uphold international law. body:
David wants to thank you for your support at this critical moment.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20251123-1&t=1&akid=2864%2E587540%2EijBZGB)
Peter, **David let us know that he wants to call grassroots donors** to thank them for supporting us during this critical moment for Leaders We Deserve.
**[Want a chance to get a call? Donate $5 now and you might be on the list.](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251123_1_calls.2bxCsZ&refcode=em20251123-2864&amount=5&t=2&akid=2864%2E587540%2EijBZGB)**

🔴 Defeating vulnerable Republicans and flipping their competitive districts blue.
🔵 Challenging ineffective Democrats in safe-seats with young, bold candidates.

But we also know that **this work is only possible with your support, Peter.** We aren’t funded by right-wing billionaires and corporate PACs. We rely on people just like you chipping in whatever amount makes sense for them, whenever they can.
If we don’t keep our fundraising on track, we risk needing to scale back on our plans for this election. _We can’t let that happen._

Leaders We Deserve

name: Adam Schiff
subject: I cannot emphasize the importance of this enough...
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC | PO Box 96047, Washington, DC 20090 | **[dscc
body:
| | | Hi -- it's Adam Schiff, DSCC Vice Chair.

**In a moment, I'm going to talk to you about something deeply important, both to me personally and to the future of our nation. But first, I have to ask:[_Will you chip in $5 right now to help flip the Senate blue and take power away from the MAGA majority as they work to enact Trump's dangerous agenda?_](https://go.dscc.org/go/22085?amount=5&t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20251123-AdamSchiff-SchiffSpecialElection-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20251123-AdamSchiff-SchiffSpecialElection-Pros-sl1&akid=21824%2E9716520%2EI4kv9z)**

I'm going to put it plain and simple -- **Democrats have a huge opportunity to pick up Senate seats -- including in a pair of special elections on the calendar.** We've got a chance to capture seats that wouldn't have been up for years -- and in this moment, after all that we've seen from Donald Trump and his lawless administration, we have to fight for every opportunity to defeat them.

As someone who has won a special election to the Senate, I cannot emphasize enough the importance of this. Democrats only need a few seats to flip the Senate majority, and with the GOP defending more seats than they were expecting, we have a real chance to catch them flat-footed.

But we can't just sit around and cross our fingers -- **we must do what we can right now to secure the resources we need to win seats like Ohio and Florida, defend blue battleground seats across the country, pick up key seats, and defeat Trump's handpicked enablers.**

**[These special elections are among a tiny handful of races that could decide the majority, so I have to ask: Will you chip in $5 to help Democrats win key elections like these and flip the Senate majority? In critical races, every dollar counts.](https://go.dscc.org/go/22085?amount=5&t=6&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20251123-AdamSchiff-SchiffSpecialElection-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20251123-AdamSchiff-SchiffSpecialElection-Pros-sl1&akid=21824%2E9716520%2EI4kv9z)**

We need your help to fight for the Senate. If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately.

Adam

Adam Schiff has represented the people of California at the state, congressional, and senatorial level for over 25 years. As a congressman, he worked to hold Trump accountable, and continues that work as a member of the United States Senate.
_**[Will you join Adam Schiff in the fight to stop Donald Trump's agenda by flipping the Senate?](https://go.dscc.org/go/22085?amount=5&t=12&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20251123-AdamSchiff-SchiffSpecialElection-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20251123-AdamSchiff-SchiffSpecialElection-Pros-sl1&akid=21824%2E9716520%2EI4kv9z)**_

name: Wes Moore
subject: We can go dollar to dollar with the Republican machine
body:
# [](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/8?t=1&akid=963%2E1086043%2ERwMMw7)
Hey Peter, it’s Wes Moore.
Now you may have heard a few of these names in the news recently: **Larry Hogan, Andy Harris, or even some guy named Ed Hale.**
It seems like one, or maybe _all of them_ , are going to be the next Republican to run against me.
**[Peter, with Republican candidates ready to jump into the race, I need your support now more than ever. Chip in $5 now to support my reelection campaign.](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251123-1.cmhsDJ&refcode=em-20251123-1&t=2&akid=963%2E1086043%2ERwMMw7)**
All eyes are on Maryland — this is not a safe blue seat. Just a few years ago this seat was held by a Republican.
Right now, we need to start preparing for these Republicans to enter the race. They have the backing of major donors, while I’m counting on the support of this grassroots army, Peter.
We need a _big_ surge of small donations right now so we can keep pace with them.
**[As Republicans cash in big checks, I am relying on grassroots supporters like you to chip in $5 or $10 whenever you can. Will you pitch in today?](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251123-1.cmhsDJ&refcode=em-20251123-1&t=3&akid=963%2E1086043%2ERwMMw7)**
**[Donate $5 ››](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251123-1.cmhsDJ&amount=5&refcode=em-20251123-1&t=22&akid=963%2E1086043%2ERwMMw7)**
Elevate,
Wes
P.S. Nothing would send a stronger message of showing that this grassroots movement can go dollar to dollar with the Republican machine **[than if you supported us with your very first donation now.](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251123-1.cmhsDJ&refcode=em-20251123-1&t=23&akid=963%2E1086043%2ERwMMw7)**

* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-footer?refcode=em-20251123-1&t=24&refcode2=963_1086043_RwMMw7&akid=963%2E1086043%2ERwMMw7)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.wesmoore.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=25&akid=963%2E1086043%2ERwMMw7)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.wesmoore.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.wesmoore.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=26&akid=963%2E1086043%2ERwMMw7)

Email us: [info@wesmoore.com](mailto:info@wesmoore.com)
BY AUTHORITY: WES MOORE FOR MARYLAND, MARY TYDINGS, TREASURER

name: The Democracy On The Line Team
subject: A brief update for Peter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Democracy on the Line PAC
body:
This will take 5 seconds

|
---

name: Katie Porter
subject: What I’d rather be doing
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=40456%2E3422442%2EjX8krT)
Peter,
I’m taking a quick break from call time (with my dog Poppy curled up by my side) to give you a quick campaign update.
[](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20251123_1_ak.u5XQnO&refcode=em20251123-40456&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=40456%2E3422442%2EjX8krT)
I have to spend more time than I’d like on the phone fundraising, because it’s very expensive to run a statewide campaign in California. We have to reach voters in every corner of the state with some of the most expensive media markets in the country.
As much as I enjoy time with Poppy, I’d rather be on the campaign trail meeting with voters and hearing their stories. That’s why I’m looking to supporters like you, Peter, to give us the boost we need.
**[Can you keep me off the phone and on the campaign trail by donating $5 today? You’ll help make sure we have the resources to reach more voters and win this race.](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20251123_1_ak.u5XQnO&refcode=em20251123-40456&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=4&akid=40456%2E3422442%2EjX8krT)**
[Donate ›››](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20251123_1_ak.u5XQnO&refcode=em20251123-40456&t=23&akid=40456%2E3422442%2EjX8krT)
Thank you,
Katie Porter

Katie is running to be California’s next governor because California and our country need a leader who will stand up to Trump to lower costs, protect our rights, and combat climate change. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4g-em-footer?refcode=em20251123-40456&amounts=&t=24&refcode2=40456_3422442_jX8krT&akid=40456%2E3422442%2EjX8krT)**
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/receive-fewer-kp4g-emails?source=40456&t=25&akid=40456%2E3422442%2EjX8krT)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/kp4g-em-update-info-footer?source=40456)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.katieporter.com/unsubscribe/kp4g-unsubscribe?source=40456&t=26&akid=40456%2E3422442%2EjX8krT)

To give by check:
Katie Porter for Governor 2026
PO Box 5176, Irvine, CA 92616
Email us: [info@katieporter.com](mailto:info@katieporter.com)

---
November 23
---
| | |
---
| | **Inside the Complex That Could Change Modern Defense**
---
| [ Exclusive Footage ](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=d8ee2d418e6c456fb4abb002441a6bb5&_e=jeaN-bPC6S74ciUh4jWeKOrVq7qcmCTsz7QaBdpCy2DadCXmj6G_Kh-dUeGdjMINf4Z4pe-5pi-ntOmF_xpMYXktVPGgxFZ8WI5_slYLMXNbxtB_S-AJfVokHq4BpLw1muznOnBwN4cD6w1OIHDFbIGKVybKOB-4XOIcDMySREh8hOU8ftVD4ewLEHw5A6CXFuHuDqBLFdsb5t04-AAw-1pzzv2Z1I0pIUaeBWqg7SLrxF3ngC1O37ZxhQKJUSvuLHrCdgMJ4pgNlxAiNAsAJ-q7dgJQkxdwNt652Np8Qaj9aZYBWK4y_9nqvV5g-hOUcYOw3AZri2KVTI98E6PnMyLcjluR-8KBRg_nq30RsAcRpT2rM6nZrTlc7opBNN0rGY_vS29h6NfM4qKgXNgi0CbIKk-8NYyTQ-mLQp7OGPmXWEv7_rDvj7MUH1TIxySiEI1HcELeVpMz9S4FUMpkNZc-npNrSERwc4gvMl-tuhonzGvPR_OJArVg_1mgy8awNhlwUWifigwDT8AqF6ZnKxR-F3tsEv5MFWfLsOGGxOl2Tg-ff2Z5jTe5JctGXemgpaBMIYrqQdKf0x5IhD6Y7Q%3D%3D)
---
| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---
Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. You’re about to see footage that’s raising eyebrows among experts worldwide.

For decades, Area 51 captured imaginations as the hub of innovation.

Now, new developments emerging from Memphis could redefine what comes next.

Tech analyst Jeff Brown was among the first to explore what’s happening at this facility.

Inside, engineers are testing machines designed to push the boundaries of current defense systems.

Brown believes this advancement could influence the entire industry—rivaling the greatest technological shifts of our era.

[Don’t miss out—watch the exclusive footage while it’s available.](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=d8ee2d418e6c456fb4abb002441a6bb5&_e=jeaN-bPC6S74ciUh4jWeKOrVq7qcmCTsz7QaBdpCy2DadCXmj6G_Kh-dUeGdjMINf4Z4pe-5pi-ntOmF_xpMYXktVPGgxFZ8WI5_slYLMXNbxtB_S-AJfVokHq4BpLw1muznOnBwN4cD6w1OIHDFbIGKVybKOB-4XOIcDMySREh8hOU8ftVD4ewLEHw5A6CXex1L5C4eg3LwyN_-fXa0iIfSi5Aw1PIfrSxgeZCoqNzy96K5zZmW0GryV7vh6w-Z-M7I0skKOOfsBIO62d8z6QZYb7imd4fwanwQ7ASCaMK5qq4uQa6pxjxcEh9uA1emTx9knmQFFxLbnJ6i0igbg1tie5ZmnIXaaC3WrDBag3pcsWsg58_bdATwT3xJqe2gf1_6heFFB4b2WtxniNaDW3GUJbIuw2jPpCTPPCKLdrgW9kNi7LCGYuxNyKmUlm1z0c5fP1brGzpuiuDgfOi6n91n9jHxH6bawbBCwFSCHJ6UqQJOWZkHApBhC3DyY0r0LbWENSXBoEUrdlj3p6zZ_q-1kCVmzyH0UTbQVNgiawVNuwMRB4mhCmRh8_S5vjrdYd37X8dc36CpZ3tX9qQDbw%3D%3D)
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. He's:**
* Driving outbreaks of deadly, vaccine-preventable diseases like measles;
* Discouraging pregnant women from taking Tylenol without any proof of harm;
* Firing thousands of world-class medicine and disease experts;
* Gutting funding for cancer research and clinical trials;
* Purging the government's vaccine review panel;
* Restricting access to lifesaving vaccines;
* _and much, much more._

That's why countless public health officials have demanded he step aside – **and likely why a recent poll found that 59% of Americans DISAPPROVE of RFK Jr.'s job performance as Trump's health secretary.**

name: Squad Victory Fund
subject: A party insider wants Ilhan’s seat
body:
[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/18910?refcode=em20251123-1638-foote&t=1001&akid=1638%2E342411%2E-yTmfP ]Squad Victory Fund

Peter,

Ilhan Omar is one of the boldest fighters in Congress — and right now,
she’s in a high-stakes battle to keep her seat.

A Democratic challenger, Latonya Reeves, is trying to take her place — but
make no mistake: Latonya is more focused on toeing the party line than
fighting for working families, justice, or real change.

Ilhan doesn’t play it safe. She fights for what truly matters: human
dignity and equality for all, peace and diplomacy, a government that works
for the working class, economic justice, Medicare for All, and access to
public education.

And she gets results: Ilhan secured $54 million in community project
funding, passed the MEALS Act, feeding nearly 30 million kids during the
pandemic, and ensured progressive climate priorities in the Inflation
Reduction Act, helping create millions of green jobs — and that’s just the
beginning.

This is a fight for the soul of progressive leadership in Congress. Ilhan
has never backed down, but she can’t do it alone.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20251123-movement.bIUPhJ&refcode=em20251123-1638&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=1638%2E342411%2E-yTmfP ]Will you chip in $3 to the Squad Victory Fund today?
Every dollar we raise now helps keep a fighter in Congress and prevents
career politicians, more interested in the status quo than change, from
taking ground.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20251123-movement.bIUPhJ&refcode=em20251123-1638&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1021&akid=1638%2E342411%2E-yTmfP ]Contribute ››

In this with you,

The Squad Victory Fund

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe:

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.squadvictoryfund.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=1638%2E342411%2E-yTmfP

Paid for by The Squad Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

In addition to what’s legally required, The Squad Victory Fund does not accept contributions from: (1) corporations, associations, or their PACs; (2) individuals registered as federal lobbyists or under FARA; (3) from the PACs, lobbyists, or SEC-named executives of fossil fuel companies; or (4) from the Fraternal Order of Police.

Contributions or gifts to The Squad Victory Fund are not tax deductible.

Contributions to The Squad Victory Fund will be allocated equally among: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

Any contributor may designate their contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if any contributor makes a contribution that, when allocated, would exceed the amount that the contributor may lawfully give to any participant.

After contributing to The Squad Victory Fund, you will automatically be signed up to receive email updates from Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, and Rashida Tlaib for Congress. You may of course opt-out by unsubscribing at any time.

To contribute via check, please address to The Squad Victory Fund c/o MBA Consulting Group, PO Box 15845, Washington D.C., 20003

Email us: info@squadvictoryfund.com

body:
President Trump's threats have real world consequences

| Friends

We're writing to share a short clip from Elissa Slotkin on Chris Hayes show Friday night, discussing President Trump's threats to arrest, prosecute, and hang her.

But before we do we're asking all supporters if they are able to make a small but meaningful donation to stand with Elissa and Hillary Scholten today.

President Trump's threats have real world consequences. Immediately after his tweetstorm, Elissa received a tidal wave of death threats, and she is now under 24 hour security protection.

But, to Elissa, this story isn't about her -- it's about all of us. It's about America. President Trump wants to use fear as a weapon to silence those who disagree with him. But we know that's contrary to what's made America an exceptional nation. And nothing will stop Elissa from standing up and speaking out to protect the country we all love.

But before we do we're asking all supporters if they are able to make a small but meaningful donation to stand with Elissa and Hillary Scholten today. President Trump's threats have real world consequences. Immediately after his tweetstorm, Elissa received a tidal wave of death threats, and she is now under 24 hour security protection. But, to Elissa, this story isn't about her -- it's about all of us. It's about America. President Trump wants to use fear as a weapon to silence those who disagree with him. But we know that's contrary to what's made America an exceptional nation. And nothing will stop Elissa from standing up and speaking out to protect the country we all love. = | | | = | = | = | = | = Now... Friday night, Elissa went on Chris Hayes to talk about President Trump's threats against her. She knows America is better than this, and we think you'll be interested in watching this clip. When you're done, please share it with your friends. The United States of America gave Elissa everything she has. She's not going to shut up just because Donald Trump is threatened by her. Thanks for standing with Elissa over the years. We wouldn't be here without it. name: Jim Stevens
subject: I’m Haley’s dad, and I’m proud as heck
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate
body:
My daughter’s running for Senate. I know this much: She’s ready.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=1&akid=1437%2E49239%2E5X6iyl)
| | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/9080?t=2&akid=1437%2E49239%2E5X6iyl)
---
[](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/9105?t=3&akid=1437%2E49239%2E5X6iyl)
Dear null,

My name is Jim Stevens. I’m a small-business owner, former public school teacher, snowplow driver, and most importantly, Haley’s dad.
I’ve worked here in Michigan for over 50 years. Still do, even though I probably should’ve retired by now. But I don’t mind hard work – never have.

And that’s the spirit I see in my daughter Haley every single day.
Whether she was helping out with the family landscaping business or staying up late studying policy before she launched her first campaign, she’s never backed down from a challenge. Grit, grime, and some Michigan doggedness – that’s Haley. She’s a fighter.

**I’ve always said that when the chips are down, Haley is exactly who you want to be standing shoulder to shoulder with.** She’s faithful to the people she loves, doesn’t have any quit in her, and always stands up for what’s right.
Now she’s in the biggest fight of her life, running for Senate, and I couldn’t be more proud. She’s in this race because she loves Michigan. She believes in Michiganders. And I know she’ll never stop fighting for working families, public school kids, and folks like you and me.
But here’s the hard truth: She’s up against millions of dollars from Republican megadonors. I’m no political expert, but I’ll tell you one thing – Haley can’t win this fight alone. That’s why I’m asking, as her dad:

**[Will you chip in $5 to help Haley win this thing and keep Michigan blue?](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/9106?t=4&akid=1437%2E49239%2E5X6iyl)**
| | [Donate Now](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/9107?t=5&akid=1437%2E49239%2E5X6iyl)
---
Haley’s not in this for fame or to build her personal brand. That’s not who she is. She’s in it for Michigan. For regular folks. And I’m with her. Always have been.

Thanks for reading this – and for standing with my daughter.
Sincerely,
Jim Stevens
|
* * *
**A note from Haley Stevens:** null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. [**If you’re in this fight with me, will you add your name to endorse my campaign?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/9079?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=6&akid=1437%2E49239%2E5X6iyl) | | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/9080?t=7&akid=1437%2E49239%2E5X6iyl)
---
You can also text “JOIN” to 80242 to become a core member of our team and receive exclusive, real-time updates from my campaign.
Thank you,
Haley
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
[To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=8&akid=1437%2E49239%2E5X6iyl)
Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate.
33717 Woodward Ave #539
Birmingham, MI 48009

name: Darializa Avila Chevalier
subject: I’m challenging Rep. Adriano Espaillat in NY-13.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251123-dac.EYQej4&refcode=em20251123-37217&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1002&akid=37217%2E1203867%2EOd9ZTE ]A photo of Darializa Avila Chevalier.

Peter,

My name is Darializa Avila Chevalier, and I'm the Justice
Democrats-endorsed candidate challenging Rep. Adriano Espaillat, AIPAC's
handpicked candidate for New York's 13th Congressional district.

We just launched this people-powered campaign, and if we’re going to take
on an AIPAC-backed, entrenched incumbent and win next June’s Democratic
primary, I need you in my corner today.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251123-dac.EYQej4&refcode=em20251123-37217&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1003&akid=37217%2E1203867%2EOd9ZTE ]New York Times headline reading: Justice Democrats Re-Emerge in New
York to Try to Unseat Espaillat. Subheadline: 'The group, which powered
Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez’s rise in 2018, is backing Darializa Avila
Chevalier’s bid to unseat Representative Adriano Espaillat.'

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251123-dac.EYQej4&refcode=em20251123-37217&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1004&akid=37217%2E1203867%2EOd9ZTE ]Will you split a contribution right now to become a founding donor of
our movement for new leadership in NY-13?

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251123-dac.EYQej4&refcode=em20251123-37217&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1005&akid=37217%2E1203867%2EOd9ZTE ]Contribute $5

This fight is already personal for me. I was doxxed for my work standing
up for human rights at Columbia University. And when Trump's paramilitary
force kidnapped my ally in this work, Mahmoud Khalil, and disappeared him
halfway across the country, I knew that our mission had expanded beyond
the scope of foreign policy and bled right into our streets.

I spent months helping elect Zohran Mamdani to become our next mayor, and
now I'm continuing the movement with this campaign for Congress.

The fight against genocide and authoritarianism has always gone hand in
hand. Our Congressman puts the will of his biggest donor, AIPAC, ahead of
our communities calling out for justice and accountability. This election,
we change that.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251123-dac.EYQej4&refcode=em20251123-37217&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1006&akid=37217%2E1203867%2EOd9ZTE ]Peter,
I hope you'll stand with me as we work to elect a leader free of the
corruption that has allowed so many lives to be destroyed. Can you join us
with a donation of any amount today?

As a community organizer, I never thought I would run for Congress. But
like so many in my community and across our country, I’ve felt left behind
by our establishment political leaders: they don’t see us for who we are
and what we need, but only show up when they need our votes.

And I know we deserve better.

As an Afro-Latina, organizer, UAW union member, and daughter of Dominican
immigrants, I know that we can’t let what we as a progressive community
built this year stop with electing Zohran.

It's that same spirit of community we'll need to channel if we want to win
this race and reshape the Democratic Party in Congress.

As a working-class candidate, I don’t have family wealth to bankroll this
campaign — and unlike my opponent, I’m not taking a dime of money from
AIPAC or corporate PACs. We’re powered by the people, and fighting for the
people. Which means we need your help.

Your grassroots support helps us feed the volunteers knocking doors with
us, power the digital outreach we'll be doing across social media, and
prepare us for the all-out campaign ahead of our June primary election.

Peter,
we're running this campaign the right way. That means no lying emails
trying to scam grandma and grandpa, no checks from anti-democratic,
pro-genocide lobbies, and no empty talking points (yes, we are going to
run on real policy!)

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251123-dac.EYQej4&refcode=em20251123-37217&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1007&akid=37217%2E1203867%2EOd9ZTE ]But in order to hit the ground running with this strategy, we need your
small-dollar support as early as possible. Can you join us as a Founding
Donor by splitting $5 between my campaign and Justice
Democrats?

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251123-dac.EYQej4&refcode=em20251123-37217&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1026&akid=37217%2E1203867%2EOd9ZTE ]Contribute $5

In solidarity,

Darializa

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20251123-37217-footer&t=1027&refcode2=37217_1203867_Od9ZTE&akid=37217%2E1203867%2EOd9ZTE

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1028&akid=37217%2E1203867%2EOd9ZTE

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.

1390 Columbia Ave #261
Lancaster, PA 17603
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

[ ]Will you split a contribution right now to become a founding donor of our movement for new leadership in NY-13? [ ]Contribute $5 This fight is already personal for me. I was doxxed for my work standing up for human rights at Columbia University. And when Trump's paramilitary force kidnapped my ally in this work, Mahmoud Khalil, and disappeared him halfway across the country, I knew that our mission had expanded beyond the scope of foreign policy and bled right into our streets. I spent months helping elect Zohran Mamdani to become our next mayor, and now I'm continuing the movement with this campaign for Congress. The fight against genocide and authoritarianism has always gone hand in hand. Our Congressman puts the will of his biggest donor, AIPAC, ahead of our communities calling out for justice and accountability. This election, we change that. [ ]Peter, I hope you'll stand with me as we work to elect a leader free of the corruption that has allowed so many lives to be destroyed. Can you join us with a donation of any amount today? As a community organizer, I never thought I would run for Congress. But like so many in my community and across our country, I’ve felt left behind by our establishment political leaders: they don’t see us for who we are and what we need, but only show up when they need our votes. And I know we deserve better. As an Afro-Latina, organizer, UAW union member, and daughter of Dominican immigrants, I know that we can’t let what we as a progressive community built this year stop with electing Zohran. It's that same spirit of community we'll need to channel if we want to win this race and reshape the Democratic Party in Congress. As a working-class candidate, I don’t have family wealth to bankroll this campaign — and unlike my opponent, I’m not taking a dime of money from AIPAC or corporate PACs. We’re powered by the people, and fighting for the people. Which means we need your help. Your grassroots support helps us feed the volunteers knocking doors with us, power the digital outreach we'll be doing across social media, and prepare us for the all-out campaign ahead of our June primary election. Peter, we're running this campaign the right way. That means no lying emails trying to scam grandma and grandpa, no checks from anti-democratic, pro-genocide lobbies, and no empty talking points (yes, we are going to run on real policy!) [ ]But in order to hit the ground running with this strategy, we need your small-dollar support as early as possible. Can you join us as a Founding Donor by splitting $5 between my campaign and Justice Democrats? [ ]Contribute $5 In solidarity, Darializa Contribute to Justice Democrats:

name: Emily Randall (personal)
subject: Silence speaks volumes
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Emily Randall for Congress
body:
A few days ago, President Trump called on several of my House colleagues to be killed.

Emily Randall for Congress
Peter, it's blowing my mind.
A few days ago, President Trump called on several of my House colleagues to be killed.
Somehow, he keeps finding a new low.
(And he has not done a single thing to lower costs for my neighbors.)
**But still, STILL, so many of my colleagues on the Republican side won’t speak out.**
Some of them will _literally say_ , “My job is to support the President’s agenda.” Nope! Your job is to be a check and balance on the Executive Branch.
That’s why I’m putting so much energy and resources into supporting candidates to replace them in 2026, and starting so early.
We’re still $7,449 short of our monthly fundraising goal, so every dollar counts. Chip in to help elect new leaders who will follow the Constitution and not be rubber stamps: _**[http://secure.actblue.com/donate/rs_er4c_fr_q42025](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119843917/584411256/1120488449?amount=25&refcode=RS_EM_FR_2025.11.23_TrumpThreats_90do_B&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmNmQ1NTdkMS05MGM4LWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=MnZl6DyPhqZoTOnLK1a73O8p2_yj2j0tOOZSBvi_hqc=)**_
I really appreciate anything you can give 💙
– Emily

We’re still $7,449 short of our monthly fundraising goal, so every dollar counts. Chip in to help elect new leaders who will follow the Constitution and not be rubber stamps: = I really appreciate anything you can give 💙 – Emily | | Paid for by Emily Randall for Congress Emily Randall for Congress P.O. Box 1883 Port Orchard, WA 98366 United States | | If you would like to receive only our most important messages, .
